name: Samoosi Yirakot (Stuffed Vegetable Turnovers)
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix the fine matzoh meal, egg, and salt together, adding enough cold water to create a moist dough that holds together. Set aside.
2. Heat corn oil in a skillet. Add chopped onion and garlic; stir-fry over moderate heat until light brown, about 3 minutes.
3. Peel and cut potato into small pieces. Chop cauliflower, carrot (1/2 cup), green peas (fresh or frozen), and thin-sliced green beans.
4. Blanch the potato and other vegetables in boiling water for 5 minutes; drain well.
5. Add the blanched vegetables to the pan with onion and garlic. Stir-fry over moderate heat for 3 minutes, mixing well.
6. Season with salt and freshly ground black pepper.
7. Take a heaping tablespoon of dough on a flat surface and press it into a square measuring about 2-1/2 inches.
8. Place a tablespoon of the vegetable mixture in the center of the dough square. Fold the dough over to form a triangle.
9. Repeat with remaining dough and filling.
10. Heat oil in a wok or skillet. Brown the turnovers over moderate heat for about 3 minutes, then drain on paper towels.
11. Serve warm. Makes about 20 turnovers.
